title: astragalus ruscifolius
intro: Astragalus ruscifolius, also known as the "Astragalus" or "Milk Vetch," is a type of perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Asia, belonging to the pea family Fabaceae. This plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immune-modulating effects. The health benefits of Astragalus ruscifolius include improved immune function, enhanced endurance, and reduced fatigue, making it a popular herb among athletes and individuals with compromised immune systems. Astragalus ruscifolius can be prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
